perturbation.original_text: Distributor shall pay for all products purchased in a timely manner.
perturbation.changed_text: Retailer shall pay for all products purchased in a timely manner.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Distributor' to 'Retailer' creates confusion because the contract consistently refers to 'Distributor' as the party contracting with Erchonia. This introduces uncertainty as to who is responsible for payment, especially since 'Retailer' is never formally defined or introduced. This change creates in-text contradiction with any other section with 'Distributor'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Distributor shall use its best efforts to market, promote and sell the Products to the authorized customers in the Filed of Use and in the Territory during the term of this agreement.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Distributor' to 'Retailer' in the payment clause creates a direct contradiction with the section outlining the 'Distributor's' responsibilities for marketing and selling the products. The original contract clearly assigns responsibilities and obligations to the 'Distributor.' By changing the payment obligation to 'Retailer,' it introduces uncertainty and conflict regarding who is bound by the contract's terms, specifically creating an in-text contradiction based on misaligned terminology.

perturbation.original_text: Erchonia agrees to provide Products to Distributor pursuant to orders placed by Distributor in the form of individual purchase orders issued by Distributor to Erchonia.
perturbation.changed_text: Erchonia agrees to provide Products to Distributor pursuant to orders placed by the Buyer in the form of individual purchase orders issued by the Purchaser to Erchonia.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'Distributor' with 'Buyer' and 'Purchaser' introduces different terms without defining them, leading to uncertainty about the ordering process. The contract now inconsistently uses 'Buyer' and 'Purchaser' in relation to order placement, contradicting the established role of the 'Distributor.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: Subject to the terms set forth in this agreement, Erchonia grants Distributor the exclusive, non- transferable right and license to promote, distribute and sell the Products identified in Exhibit A to those type of customer specified in Exhibit B and only within the Territory specified in Exhibit B. Distributor shall only distribute or sell the Products to customers who are licensed health care professionals and meet the other requirements set forth in Exhibit B.
perturbation.justification: The modified text replaces 'Distributor' with 'Buyer' and 'Purchaser'. The contradicted text states that 'Erchonia grants Distributor the exclusive, non-transferable right and license to promote, distribute and sell the Products'. By changing 'Distributor' to 'Buyer' and 'Purchaser' in the ordering process (changed text), and not consistently throughout the document, the contract now uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them. This leads to conflicting obligations and uncertainty about who has the right to distribute and sell products, thus creating an in-text contradiction as defined by Misaligned Terminology.

perturbation.original_text: Distributor shall not modify nor create or attempt to create, by reverse engineering or otherwise, the Products supplied hereunder, or adapt the Products in any way for other uses without Erchonia's prior written consent
perturbation.changed_text: The Sales Team shall not modify nor create or attempt to create, by reverse engineering or otherwise, the Products supplied hereunder, or adapt the Products in any way for other uses without Erchonia's prior written consent
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Distributor' to 'The Sales Team' creates an internal contradiction. While the original contract gives obligations of the distributor as a whole unit, the replacement will make it sound like its only restricting 'The Sales Team' from modifying or creating the Products, and no one else. The change creates in-text contradiction with any other section with 'Distributor'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Distributor agrees that during the term of this agreement it meet the minimum performance goals set forth in Exhibit C to this agreement. Failure to meet these minimum performance goals for any period, shall, at Erchonia's option (i) be considered a breach of this agreement for which Erchonia shall have all the rights and remedies provided for herein upon a breach of this agreement, including termination of this agreement, or (ii) shall give Erchonia to terminate or limit the exclusivity provisions of this agreement
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the term 'Distributor' to 'The Sales Team'. The original text placed obligations on the Distributor as a whole, while the changed text only restricts 'The Sales Team' from modifying or creating products. The contradicted text obligates the 'Distributor' to meet certain performance goals. Because 'Distributor' is now narrowed to 'The Sales Team' in a previous clause, the contradicted text now introduces ambiguity and makes it unclear whether the sales team is solely responsible for meeting the performance goals or if this is a broader company-wide requirement. This constitutes a strong contradiction as defined by the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type.
